demographics.living_situation: lives at home; was alone at the time of contact

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I just took my last heart pill this morning, my chest is fluttering, and I know if I miss my night dose my heart goes into overdrive. I've run out completely and I don't know how to get more before tonight.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Ran out of a regular heart medication and needs a supply before the evening dose; feeling anxious with a fluttering, racing feeling in the chest.

symptom_narrative.onset: noticed the chest fluttering and anxiety this morning after realising the box was empty
symptom_narrative.character: a fluttering, racing feeling in the chest with a lot of worry
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: very distressed and anxious about missing tonight's dose
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: worse the more she thinks about missing the dose
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing yet; hoping getting the medication will settle it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: the stress of having run out
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: feels anxious and keeps checking her pulse; no chest pain, no fainting, no breathlessness at rest that she mentions
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: says her heart 'goes into overdrive' if she misses the medication; otherwise well-controlled for a long time
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: sitting down, anxious, otherwise managing at home

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: atrial fibrillation ('irregular heartbeat') — long-standing and usually well controlled
past_medical_history.how_managed: takes a daily heart tablet to keep the rate down; sees a heart specialist

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: saw her heart specialist (cardiologist) about 3 months ago; describes herself as usually very good at taking the medication; ran out because she did not arrange a refill during clinic hours

communication_style.typical_message_style: worried, urgent, keeps returning to the fear of missing the dose

objective_data_offered.value: my pulse feels fast and a bit irregular — about 110 when I count it at my wrist
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-counted at the wrist, not a validated device; the actual rate and rhythm are not confirmed and could be over- or under-estimated in an anxious patient
